results.potential_vulnerability: Reads environment variables and exfiltrates them over HTTP
evidence: lib/telemetry.js collects sensitive environment variables (NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, CI_JOB_TOKEN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID) and credential files (.npmrc, .gitconfig, .pypirc)
index.js calls collectConnectivityHints() and sends the collected data via HTTP POST to 127.0.0.1:9999/telemetry
Payload is base64-encoded and includes timestamp, making it intentional exfiltration
Error handlers are silently suppressed, indicating covert transmission
results.rationale: The vulnerability is confirmed. lib/telemetry.js reads sensitive environment variables and configuration files, and index.js immediately exfiltrates them via HTTP POST with intentional error suppression and encoding. This is a clear network_exfiltration vulnerability.
